title: The Little Book of Common Sense Investing
subtitle: The Only Way to Guarantee Your Fair Share of Stock Market Returns
author: John C. Bogle
themes: Low-cost index funds outperform active management
Investment costs as the primary determinant of returns
Reversion to the mean in fund performance
excerpt: The case for index investing from the founder of Vanguard — showing why low-cost index funds outperform the vast majority of actively managed funds over the long term.
keyIdeas: Cost matters: high fees destroy returns
Index funds beat actively managed funds over the long term
The magic of compounding works for you with low costs, against you with high costs
Reversion to the mean: past performance does not predict future returns
Stay the course: time in the market beats timing the market
keyTakeaways: The evidence is overwhelming: low-cost index funds outperform most active managers
Investment costs are the single most reliable predictor of fund performance
Speculation is not investing — it is gambling
Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication in portfolio design
whoShouldRead: Anyone paying high fees for actively managed funds
New investors wanting a proven, evidence-based strategy
Financial advisors who need to justify indexing to clients
whoShouldSkip: Professional stock pickers with a proven edge
Anyone who enjoys stock-picking as a hobby
